subsection.heading: Director of Commercial High-Performance Green Buildings
subsection.chapeau: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary, acting through the Assistant Secretary of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy, shall appoint a Director of Commercial High-Performance Green Buildings to a position in the career-reserved Senior Executive service, with the principal responsibility to—

paragraph.content: establish and manage the Office of Commercial High-Performance Green Buildings; and
carry out other duties as required under this part.

content.p: The Commercial Director shall be an individual, who by reason of professional background and experience, is specifically qualified to carry out the duties required under this part.

subsection.heading: Duties
subsection.chapeau: The Commercial Director shall, with respect to development of high-performance green buildings and zero-energy commercial buildings nationwide—

paragraph.content: coordinate the activities of the Office of Commercial High-Performance Green Buildings with the activities of the Office of Federal High-Performance Green Buildings;

develop the legal predicates and agreements for, negotiate, and establish one or more public-private partnerships with the Consortium, members of the Consortium, and other capable parties meeting the qualifications of the Consortium, to further such development;
represent the public and the Department in negotiating and performing in accord with such public-private partnerships;
use appropriated funds in an effective manner to encourage the maximum investment of private funds to achieve such development;
promote research and development of high-performance green buildings, consistent with section 17083 of this title; and

paragraph.chapeau: jointly establish with the Federal Director a national high-performance green building clearinghouse in accordance with section 17083(1) of this title, which shall provide high-performance green building information and disseminate research results through—

content.p: The Commercial Director shall report directly to the Assistant Secretary for Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy, or to other senior officials in a way that facilitates the integrated program of this part for both energy efficiency and renewable energy and both technology development and technology deployment.

subsection.heading: Coordination
subsection.chapeau: The Commercial Director shall ensure full coordination of high-performance green building information and activities, including activities under this part, within the Federal Government by working with the General Services Administration and all relevant agencies, including, at a minimum—

paragraph.content: the Office of the Federal Environmental Executive;
the Office of Federal Procurement Policy;
the Department of Energy, particularly the Federal Energy Management Program;
the Department of Health and Human Services;
the Department of Housing and Urban Development;
the National Institute of Standards and Technology;
the Office of Science Technology and Policy; and
such nonprofit high-performance green building rating and analysis entities as the Commercial Director determines can offer support, expertise, and review services.

content.p: Not later than 90 days after December 19, 2007, the Commercial Director shall formally recognize one or more groups that qualify as a high-performance green building partnership consortium.

paragraph.heading: Representation to qualify
paragraph.chapeau: To qualify under this section, any consortium shall include representation from—

subparagraph.content: the design professions, including national associations of architects and of professional engineers;
the development, construction, financial, and real estate industries;
building owners and operators from the public and private sectors;
academic and research organizations, including at least one national laboratory with extensive commercial building energy expertise;
building code agencies and organizations, including a model energy code-setting organization;
independent high-performance green building associations or councils;
experts in indoor air quality and environmental factors;
experts in intelligent buildings and integrated building information systems;
manufacturers and providers of equipment and techniques used in high-performance green buildings;

content.p: The Secretary may make payments to the Consortium pursuant to the terms of a public-private partnership for such activities of the Consortium undertaken under such a partnership as described in this part directly to the Consortium or through one or more of its members.

subsection.heading: Report
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 2 years after December 19, 2007, and biennially thereafter, the Commercial Director, in consultation with the Consortium, shall submit to Congress a report that—

paragraph.chapeau: describes the status of the high-performance green building initiatives under this part and other Federal programs affecting commercial high-performance green buildings in effect as of the date of the report, including—

subparagraph.content: the extent to which the programs are being carried out in accordance with this part; and
the status of funding requests and appropriations for those programs; and

paragraph.content: summarizes and highlights development, at the State and local level, of high-performance green building initiatives, including executive orders, policies, or laws adopted promoting high-performance green building (including the status of implementation of those initiatives).

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective on the date that is 1 day after Dec. 19, 2007, see section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140, set out as a note under section 1824 of Title 2, The Congress.

note.heading: Change of Name
note.p: Office of the Federal Environmental Executive reestablished as the Office of the Chief Sustainability Officer by Ex. Ord. No. 13693, § 6, Mar. 19, 2015, 80 F.R. 15877, formerly set out in a note under section 4321 of this title.
